shortAnswer: fT3 (free triiodothyronine) is the active thyroid hormone that influences metabolism, heart rate, and many body functions. Elevated levels may indicate hyperthyroidism, while low levels may point to hypothyroidism. Discuss any deviations with your doctor.
metaDescription: fT3 elevated or low? Normal ranges, meaning, and causes of free triiodothyronine abnormalities: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: How should my fT3 be interpreted in relation to fT4 and TSH?
Do I need a thyroid scan?
pageTitle: fT3 value: Free Triiodothyronine explained | Medyra
